summary: Relax the `Contract` model's required internal `name` to optional, drop the `MissingName` error path, and derive a display label from the contract file stem â€” resolving (contract-name-field).

per.section: Decision: a contract is identified by its path/role, not an internal name

edit.description: A contract's identity is its path/role, not an internal name (spec Decision). Relax `Contract.name` from `String` to `Option<String>` (`None` when the file declares none â€” the curated template's case, contract.rs:46-47). Remove the `MissingName` variant (contract.rs:207-213) and the parse-time required-`name` check that raises it (contract.rs:315-321), so a nameless contract loads. Provide a display label for diagnostics derived from the contract file stem (the path is already threaded into `parse`, contract.rs:306) â€” e.g. `skill.anthropic` for `contracts/skill.anthropic.toml`. Update the in-module unit tests: drop `missing_name_is_a_load_error` (contract.rs:866-875), and adjust `rep_expected`/`a_contract_with_no_clauses_is_vacuously_valid`/the REP fixture's `name` expectation to the new optional shape.

Test-only: repoint the `#[cfg(test)]` `Contract { name: ... }` construction sites to the new optional-name shape â€” the `contract()` helper (engine.rs:299-307) and the two inline `Contract { name: "skill".to_string(), clauses }` literals (~engine.rs:567, ~engine.rs:618). No engine behavior change; `validate` already reads only `contract.clauses`, never `name`.

tests.asserts: a contract declaring no `name` parses (no `MissingName`); `Contract.name` is `Option<String>`; the REP fixture and vacuous-contract tests reflect the optional shape; a display label derives from the file stem
the engine's unit suite still passes against the optional-name `Contract` (construction sites only â€” no decision-table change)

acceptance: `Contract.name` is `Option<String>`; `Contract::parse` no longer errors on an absent `name` (the curated `contracts/skill.anthropic.toml` would load); the `MissingName` variant is gone; a diagnostic display label derives from the contract file stem; `cargo test` / `clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Resolves (contract-name-field) option B â€” the model's required `name` was code drift; the spec identifies a contract by its path/role. Unblocks SKILL-CONTRACT-TEMPLATE â†’ CHECK-CUTOVER (both `include_str!`/parse the nameless template). Touches contract.rs (model + its tests) and engine.rs test construction sites only; disjoint from the downstream entries.

summary: Pin the shipped Anthropic skill template (contracts/skill.anthropic.toml, human-authored) by loading it through `contract::Contract` and asserting its surviving decidable clause vector.

per.section: Templates â€” best practices as data

new.description: Load the bundled `contracts/skill.anthropic.toml` through `contract::Contract::load` (or `include_str!` + `Contract::parse`) and assert it parses into the model carrying exactly the surviving decidable clauses at their declared severities: `name` required + min_len 1 + allowed_chars `[a-z0-9-]` + max_len 64 + deny `[anthropic, claude]`; `name-matches-dir`; `description` required + min_len 1 + max_len 1024; body `max_lines` 500 (advisory); `forbidden_keys` `[globs, alwaysApply]`. Assert the contract carries no internal `name` (identity is its path; display label derives from the `skill.anthropic` stem). No dropped heuristic (third-person/trigger/companion-refs) appears.

tests.asserts: the shipped template deserializes into the Contract model with no internal `name` and declares the expected clause set (name format/length/deny, description length, body max_lines advisory, forbidden_keys, name-matches-dir) at the right severities

acceptance: `contracts/skill.anthropic.toml` loads into the Contract model and encodes only the decidable surviving clauses; the dropped heuristics are absent; `cargo test` green.
notes: Data file is DONE â€” human-authored as curated guidance (6e0af11); build EMBEDS contracts/, never writes it. Test-only now. Was fork-held on (contract-name-field) because the nameless template tripped the old `MissingName`; CONTRACT-NAME-OPTIONAL removes that, so this is now an ordinary blockedBy.

summary: Wire `temper check` to load the built-in skill contract and run the generic engine over extracted Features; add `--deny-advisories` to promote advisories to blocking.

edit.description: Check loads the workspace, extracts `extract::skill_features` over each `ws.skills` entry into a `Vec<Features>`, validates against the built-in skill contract (`include_str!("../contracts/skill.anthropic.toml")` parsed by `contract::Contract::parse`) via `engine::validate`, renders via `check::render`, and exits non-zero when `check::any_error` (a required-clause violation) fires; add a `--deny-advisories` flag so advisory (`Warn`) violations also fail. Stop calling `rules::all_rules` and drop the `use temper::rules;` import (main.rs:16,57). While rewriting dispatch, refresh the now-inaccurate module doc: the header (main.rs:1-9) narrates the rule-registration step this entry removes and cites the absent `spec/RELEASE-v0.1.md` (main.rs:4), and the `DEFAULT_WORKSPACE` doc comment cites it again (main.rs:19-21) â€” repoint both to the evergreen `specs/` corpus (`00-intent.md` evergreen-spec decision), the main.rs twin of the lib.rs doc scrub.

keep the clean/error-exit cases (name-format + name-matches-dir still fire as required-clause errors); add a `--deny-advisories` case where an advisory-only workspace exits non-zero with the flag and zero without it. While editing, repoint this file's stale module-doc header (cli.rs:1, citing the absent `spec/RELEASE-v0.1.md`) to the evergreen `specs/` corpus.

tests.asserts: import->check exits 0 on a clean skill, non-zero on an uppercase/mismatched name; `--deny-advisories` flips an advisory-only run to non-zero

acceptance: The gate now runs the declared contract, not the registry; default blocks on `required` only, `--deny-advisories` also blocks on advisory; `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Depends on SKILL-EXTRACTOR + CONTRACT-ENGINE (both shipped) + SKILL-CONTRACT-TEMPLATE (the include_str! target). Embeds the bundled template as the default; override/selection is the (contract-selection) fork. Leaves `rules.rs` + `check::Rule`/`check::run` in place (still public, no dead-code warning) for RETIRE-HEURISTICS.

summary: Delete the rejected heuristic rule registry and its dead fixtures/tests; move the acceptance snapshot onto the contract-engine path.

per.section: Decision: kill the heuristic rule registry

edit.description: remove `pub mod rules;` (lib.rs:23, this entry's line only)

drive `engine::validate` against the built-in skill contract instead of `all_rules()`; regenerate the diagnostic-set snapshot to the reduced surviving-clause set (import surface snapshot unchanged). While rewriting, repoint the stale module-doc header (acceptance.rs:1) citing the absent `spec/RELEASE-v0.1.md` to the evergreen `specs/` corpus.

tests.asserts: import->validate->re-import over the contract path reproduces the surviving diagnostics with no surface diff; the dropped heuristics no longer appear

acceptance: `src/rules.rs`, `tests/rules.rs`, and the five dropped-heuristic fixtures are gone; `lib.rs` has no `rules` module; acceptance runs on the engine; regenerated snapshot reflects the decidable clauses only; `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Scope honesty: this removes shipped behavior (third-person, has-trigger, has-anti-trigger, prose-grep companion-refs, refs-one-level-deep) â€” mandated by the corpus, which names trigger + companion-refs as false-positive sources. The decidable members survive as template clauses, not code. Leaves the now-callerless `check::Rule`/`check::run` for a follow-up tick to reconcile.
